title: Bridge Essay: Neural Effects of One's Own Voice on Self-Talk for Emotion Regulation.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Phase Language/Mantra bridge-v3 targeted acquisition.

**Neural Effects of One's Own Voice on Self-Talk for Emotion Regulation.** — Brain sciences (2024). PMID: 39061378. DOI: 10.3390/brainsci14070637. Score: 47. One'sown voice undergoes unique processing that distinguishes it from others' voices, and thus listening to it may have aspecial neural basis for self-talk as an emotion regulation strategy. This study aimed to elucidate how neural effects of one'sown voice differ from those of others' voices on the implementation of emotion regulation strategies.
